lede: A look at July 28 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Mathieu Debuchy and Marcel Duchamp, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Provisional Irish Republican Army
events.desc: The Provisional Irish Republican Army calls an end to its thirty-year-long armed campaign against British rule in Northern Ireland.

events.title: Olympic Games
events.desc: Olympic Games: Games of the XXIII Olympiad: The summer Olympics were opened in Los Angeles.

events.title: 1976 Tangshan earthquake
events.desc: The Tangshan earthquake measuring between 7.8 and 8.2 moment magnitude flattens Tangshan in the People's Republic of China, killing 242,769 and injuring 164,851.

events.title: North American B-25 Mitchell
events.desc: A U.S. Army B-25 bomber crashes into the 79th floor of the Empire State Building killing 14 and injuring 26.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Soviet leader Joseph Stalin issues Order No. 227. In response to alarming German advances, all those who retreat or otherwise leave their positions without orders to do so are to be tried in a military court, with punishment ranging from duty in a shtrafbat battalion, imprisonment in a Gulag, or execution.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: In the culmination of the July Crisis, Austria-Hungary declares war on the Kingdom of Serbia and begins the Great War.

events.title: Australasian Antarctic Expedition
events.desc: The Australasian Antarctic Expedition began as the SY Aurora departed London.

events.title: Vinnie Ream
events.desc: At the age of 18, Vinnie Ream becomes the first and youngest female artist to receive a commission from the United States government for a statue (of Abraham Lincoln).

events.title: Cantabria
events.desc: Constitution of the province of Cantabria ratified at the Assembly Hall in Bárcena la Puente, Reocín, Spain.

events.title: Henry VIII
events.desc: Henry VIII of England marries his fifth wife, Catherine Howard.

births.name: Hugo Chávez
births.line: Venezuelan colonel and politician, President of Venezuela

births.name: William T. Vollmann
births.line: American novelist, short story writer and journalist

deaths.text: Joseph Bonaparte, French diplomat and brother of Napoleon
Louis Antoine de Saint-Just, French soldier and politician
Johann Sebastian Bach, German organist and composer

observances.text: Christian feast day: Alphonsa Muttathupadathu (Syro-Malabar Catholic Church)
Christian feast day: Johann Sebastian Bach, George Frederick Handel, Henry Purcell (Episcopal Church commemoration)
Christian feast day: Johann Sebastian Bach, Heinrich Schütz, George Frederick Handel (Lutheran commemoration)
